author: super-dev
gameControls: [WASD] Move · [Mouse] Look · [Click] Fire · [R / Right-click] Reload · [1–8 / Wheel] Weapon · [E] Use
bio: Full-stack .NET / Angular dev with a DevOps streak on Azure Cloud. Also fluent in Flutter + Firebase. I build products, ship them, and keep them healthy.
descriptionBody: Welcome to my channel. I document what I build: .NET APIs, Angular UIs, Azure DevOps pipelines, and the occasional Flutter app. This video is a fast tour of what I do. Chapters are below the timeline. — open to full-stack or platform missions.

comments.body: We worked together on the Azure migration. Solid on DevOps and ships clean Angular — rare combo. Recommended.
Code reviews with him = masterclass. Strong architecture, real test coverage, and he says no when no is the right answer.
Takes a fuzzy brief and turns it into a clean sprint. Even rarer: shows up to standup with solutions, not just blockers.
Clean Azure DevOps pipelines, sane IaC. Cut our deploy time by 40% thanks to his refactor.

projects.title: $ flutter run — cross-platform mobile app with Firebase
// dev stream — building a GraphQL API in .NET live

articles.title: A DOOM-style engine in the browser, with three backends
articles.description: A DOOM-style BSP software renderer, no WebGL: three interchangeable backends (CPU, workers, WebGPU) and a test proving they render the same pixel.

articles.title: Strangle a .NET 8 monolith without breaking everything
articles.description: The Strangler Fig pattern to carve a .NET monolith into reversible deliveries, with an anti-corruption layer and a progressive cutover.

articles.title: Take an Angular app zoneless + signals
articles.description: Drop zone.js, adopt signals and fine-grained reactivity, and make change detection rock-solid in a real Angular 21 app.

articles.title: Deploy a prerendered (SSG) Angular SPA to Azure Static Web Apps
articles.description: Native Angular prerender (outputMode static), sitemap/robots/llms.txt and serverless deployment to Azure Static Web Apps.

articles.title: Loading data with resource() and httpResource()
articles.description: Angular 21's reactive primitives for async data, no manual subscribe.

articles.title: A GitHub Actions → Azure CI/CD pipeline, from scratch
articles.description: Continuous build, test, and deployment to Azure from GitHub Actions — secrets, environments and guardrails included.

articles.title: Deploying a .NET API to Azure Container Apps
articles.description: Ingress, KEDA scale-to-zero and revisions: containerized serverless.

articles.title: Offline-first sync in Flutter + Firebase
articles.description: A Flutter app that works offline: Firestore cache, a write queue and conflict resolution.

articles.title: Multi-stage Docker images for .NET + Angular
articles.description: Build/runtime stages, layer caching and tiny final images.

articles.title: @defer and the new control flow: less JS at startup
articles.description: @if/@for/@defer to load UI on demand and shrink the initial bundle.

articles.title: Tutorial — testing zoneless Angular components with Vitest
articles.description: Configure Vitest for zoneless Angular and test signals, inputs and templates without a heavy TestBed.

articles.title: A testable Flutter architecture with Riverpod
articles.description: Providers, notifiers and test overrides: separate UI from logic.

articles.title: Minimal APIs + EF Core: a clean .NET 8 API
articles.description: Endpoints, route groups, EF Core and validation: a minimal yet testable .NET 8 API.

articles.title: App state with NgRx SignalStore
articles.description: withState/withComputed/withMethods: a reactive store, 100% signals.

articles.title: CQRS and vertical slices without the ceremony
articles.description: Split commands from queries per feature, without over-engineering.

articles.title: .NET observability with OpenTelemetry
articles.description: Traces, metrics and logs via OTLP and the Collector.

articles.title: Zero-config secrets: Key Vault + Managed Identity
articles.description: No more secrets in config: DefaultAzureCredential and RBAC.

articles.title: .NET microservices with gRPC
articles.description: A .proto contract, typed client, streaming: when gRPC beats REST.

articles.title: Tutorial — mastering interactive rebase
articles.description: Squash, reword, reorder and the golden rule, without breaking shared history.

articles.title: A Flutter monorepo with Melos
articles.description: Split into packages, melos.yaml scripts and CI versioning.

articles.title: Generating code at compile time: source generators
articles.description: Incremental generators that replace runtime reflection.

series.title: Modern .NET
series.description: Minimal APIs, CQRS, gRPC, source generators: clean, testable .NET 8 without the ceremony.

series.title: Angular 21 in practice
series.description: Zoneless, signals, resource(), @defer, SignalStore — the modern front end, for real.

series.title: Azure DevOps from scratch
series.description: Prerender, Container Apps, secrets, CI/CD, Docker, observability: ship to Azure without a server or the pain.

series.title: Flutter in production
series.description: Offline-first, Riverpod, a Melos monorepo: serious mobile, not a prototype.

seriesUi.title: Series
seriesUi.subtitle: Articles grouped by theme — beginner to advanced
seriesUi.empty: No articles published in this series yet.

sceneIntro.name: super-dev
sceneIntro.tagline: I design, I code, I deploy, I monitor.

projectScenes.name: Multi-tenant B2B SaaS platform
projectScenes.description: Rebuilt a legacy ASP.NET WebForms product into a modern SaaS. Azure AD B2C auth, Stripe billing, BI exports.

projectScenes.name: Cross-platform mobile app
projectScenes.description: Field service app for techs. Offline-first, incremental sync, push, geotagged photos.

projectScenes.name: Monolith → microservices migration
projectScenes.description: Strangler pattern over 18 months. Domain split, event-driven via Service Bus, AKS production.

projectScenes.name: Multi-env CI/CD pipelines
projectScenes.description: Azure DevOps Pipelines + IaC Terraform/Bicep. Dev → staging → prod with approvals and auto rollback.

sceneOutro.hash: // end of tour. thanks for watching.
sceneOutro.subtitle: Open to new missions.
sceneOutro.cta: If you need a dev who codes, ships, and knows why the pipeline is red — we should talk.

about.heading: $ cat about.md
about.subtitle: who I am, what I'm after, how to reach me
paragraphs: My name is super-dev. I've been a full-stack developer for 9 years, with a DevOps streak that grew gradually — first out of necessity, then out of genuine pleasure for shipping things properly.
Day-to-day: C# / .NET for APIs and services, TypeScript / Angular for interfaces, Bicep + Terraform on Azure. And regularly, Dart / Flutter when a mobile project shows up.

I believe in frequent releases, tests that actually cover, pipelines that don't lie, and the fact that "it works on my machine" is not a proof. My colleagues describe me as someone who ships and who teaches.

details.label: Availability
details.value: Open to missions — freelance or permanent

levels.tierSubtitle: 5+ years, recurring prod use, I can debug blindfolded

techs.name: .NET / C#
techs.note: From Framework 4.x to .NET 8. Prefer Minimal APIs on greenfield.

techs.name: Angular
techs.note: From AngularJS to v21 + signals. Daily RxJS.

techs.name: Azure App Service
techs.note: Simplest fit for 80% of cases. AKS when needed.

techs.name: Azure DevOps
techs.note: YAML pipelines, service connections, multi-env release flows.

techs.name: SQL Server / EF Core
techs.note: EF Core 8 + migrations. Dapper when raw speed matters.

levels.tierSubtitle: 2-5 years, shipped to prod, I know what breaks

techs.name: AKS / Kubernetes
techs.note: Helm, KEDA for event-driven scaling. Not an SRE expert.

techs.name: Terraform / Bicep
techs.note: Reusable modules, Azure Storage state. Prefer Bicep for pure Azure.

techs.name: Flutter / Dart
techs.note: Riverpod, GoRouter, custom rendering. 2 apps in stores.

techs.name: Firebase
techs.note: Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions, FCM. Known limits.

techs.name: Service Bus / RabbitMQ
techs.note: Event-driven via MassTransit. Sagas, retry, DLQ.

levels.tierSubtitle: less than a year cumulative, but I adapt fast

techs.name: Python / FastAPI
techs.note: ETL scripts, internal micro-APIs. Not my main stack.

techs.name: GraphQL
techs.note: HotChocolate server-side, Apollo client-side. Pick your battles.

techs.name: Docker / containers
techs.note: Daily for dev, multi-stage builds, healthchecks.

techs.name: Postgres
techs.note: When escaping SQL Server. JSON columns, full-text.
